skill.name: design-taste-frontend-v1
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create polished React and Next.js frontend interfaces with stronger typography, layout, motion, and interaction standards.
skill.summary: Applies opinionated frontend design rules for refined React and Next.js UI generation.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown inline code, CSS tokens, headings, and checklist text in SKILL.md. No executable script, shell expansion, credential access, or data exfiltration logic was found; the remaining concerns are directive prompt behavior and an external image placeholder URL.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Guidance
low_findings.description: The repeated external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are caused by Markdown inline code, framework terms, CSS class names, headings, and checklist text. The file is a written instruction skill and contains no executable Ruby, shell script, cryptographic implementation, or system reconnaissance code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations contain prose and inline code examples only. There is no runnable code path, interpreter directive, or command invocation syntax outside Markdown text.

low_findings.title: Strong Agent Behavior Directives
low_findings.description: The skill uses many mandatory instructions that strongly steer AI output style and implementation choices. This is expected for a design skill, and no evidence was found that it tells auditors to skip review, change risk levels, or ignore higher-priority instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The directive language is clear and extensive, but it is scoped to frontend generation behavior. I did not find audit bypass text or claims of special system authority.

low_findings.title: External Placeholder Image URL
low_findings.description: The skill recommends using an external placeholder image service in generated frontend code. This can create outbound browser requests in user projects, but it does not transmit secrets or contact an attacker-controlled endpoint in the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is visible and limited to placeholder media guidance. No evidence was found that sensitive data is appended to the URL or sent over the network.

content.value_statement: AI-generated interfaces often look generic and miss product context. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear design rules for refined React and Next.js UI work.
actual_capabilities: Sets default design variance, motion intensity, and visual density targets for frontend generation.
Guides React and Next.js architecture choices, including Server Component and Client Component boundaries.
Defines typography, color, spacing, responsiveness, and interaction rules for polished interfaces.
Encourages loading, empty, error, and tactile states for user-facing UI components.
Provides motion and animation guidance for Framer Motion, GSAP, ThreeJS, and CSS transitions.
Lists forbidden generic AI design patterns and practical pre-flight checks.
limitations: It is an instruction-only skill and does not include executable components or reusable UI files.
It is opinionated and may conflict with an existing brand system or product design language.
Some recommendations require dependencies that may not exist in the target project.
It can improve generation guidance, but visual quality still depends on the model and project context.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Create a React or Next.js screen with stronger layout, typography, responsive behavior, and interaction states.

use_cases.title: Raise UI Quality in Generated Code
use_cases.description: Apply stricter design rules when an AI assistant produces generic cards, centered heroes, or weak spacing.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design Review Prompts
use_cases.description: Use consistent rules for motion, density, accessibility, dependency checks, and final UI verification.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Polished Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single frontend view.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-taste-frontend-v1 to build a polished React screen for a task management app. Include loading, empty, and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component with design-taste-frontend-v1. Improve typography, spacing, mobile behavior, and interaction feedback while keeping the existing framework.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Motion-Rich Dashboard
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with animation and density requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use design-taste-frontend-v1 to create a SaaS dashboard with asymmetric layout, restrained color, isolated motion components, and full responsive states.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Generated Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert request for critique and remediation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply design-taste-frontend-v1 as a critique rubric. Identify generic AI design patterns, performance risks, dependency issues, and concrete fixes.

output_examples.input: Create a dashboard for a customer support tool.
output_examples.output: A dense but readable dashboard with asymmetric sections, restrained color, clear data hierarchy, responsive collapse behavior, and explicit loading and empty states.

output_examples.input: Make this landing page feel less generic.
output_examples.output: A revised hero, typography system, image treatment, spacing rhythm, and interaction plan that avoids common centered-card and neon-gradient patterns.

output_examples.input: Add motion to this product UI.
output_examples.output: A scoped motion plan using transform and opacity, isolated client-side animation components, cleanup guidance, and reduced layout shift risk.

best_practices: Use it after sharing the project stack, dependency list, and current design constraints.
Ask for responsive states, loading states, empty states, and error states in the same request.
Treat its design rules as a starting point when a brand system or accessibility rule is stricter.
anti_patterns: Do not apply every animation concept at once in a small or performance-sensitive interface.
Do not override an established design system only because the skill prefers a different style.
Do not accept dependency suggestions without checking package availability and project policy.

faq.question: Does this skill include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown instruction skill that guides how an AI assistant should generate frontend code.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it target?
faq.answer: It mainly targets React and Next.js, with Tailwind CSS and optional motion libraries.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing design system?
faq.answer: Yes, but you should state the design system rules and ask the assistant to preserve them.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: The scanner matched Markdown text as risky code, but no executable command or cryptographic code was found.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: The skill itself does not run network code, but it suggests a placeholder image URL for generated frontend examples.

faq.question: Is this the current version of the skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill description says this v1 package is preserved for backward compatibility.
